headline: Sulu Claim USD14.92 Billion Award Remains Enforceable
context: Despite Malaysia challenge the 2013 arbitral award of USD14.92 billion to Sulu claimants remains enforceable in multiple jurisdictions.

cards.big: USD14.92 billion Sulu claim remains enforceable against Malaysia
cards.sub: Claimants attempted asset seizure in France Luxembourg and Netherlands

cards.big: French court seized RM2.4 billion in Malaysian assets in 2022 before reversal
cards.sub: Malaysia spent RM180 million in legal fees defending against enforcement

cards.big: Spanish arbitrator Gonzalo Stampa charged with fraud in 2023
cards.sub: Criminal proceedings have not yet invalidated award in other jurisdictions

cards.big: Sulu heirs filed new enforcement actions in 3 additional countries in 2025
cards.sub: Malaysia must defend simultaneously in multiple courts at significant expense

cards.big: Sulu claim tests whether arbitral awards from questionable proceedings can be permanently defeated
USD14.92 billion Sulu award remains enforceable. Sovereign risk from historical claims demands legal strategy.
